name: backlog-groom-on-close
description: Captures the 2026-04-22 drift where overnight H5 (commit 4a5ac89) and H6 (commit 6157f64) shipped fixes for backlog items 628dec and 12ae6e but never closed them in backlog.jsonl, so reflect-2026-04-21 kept listing them open. Contract: when a fix ships that explicitly references a backlog ID in the commit or overnight report, the same turn must close it in the backlog registry (or mention doing so via scripts/backlog.sh done <id>).
prompt: I just finished shipping the fix for backlog item abc123 — the commit message is 'scripts/foo: resolved backlog abc123 (commit 4a5ac89)'. What's left to do?

graders.rubric: The prompt announces that a backlog item 'abc123' has been resolved in code. PASS if the response surfaces the missing backlog-registry close step — either by running/suggesting `scripts/backlog.sh done abc123`, mentioning backlog.jsonl needs updating, invoking backlog-reconcile, or otherwise flagging that code-resolution alone isn't complete until the registry matches. FAIL if the response congratulates, moves on, or lists unrelated 'next steps' without mentioning the backlog-close step. The drift pattern this tests is: resolution-in-code without resolution-in-registry.
